outbound_official.notes: Taiwan's Bureau of Consular Affairs (BOCA), Ministry of Foreign Affairs, publishes the official outbound visa-free list at https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-149-4486-7785a-2.html. As of May 12, 2025, 173 countries and territories in total accord visa-free entry, landing visas, or e-visas to ROC (Taiwan) ordinary passport holders (per taiwan.gov.tw). The BOCA page explicitly lists visa-free destinations only; BOCA does not publish a consolidated single-page list of outbound VOA or e-visa destinations for Taiwan nationals - those must be verified country by country via destination-side sources. Time-limited visa-free schemes: Thailand, Brunei, Philippines valid until July 31, 2026; North Macedonia until March 31, 2030. Stay limits vary: most 90-day; Belize, Dominican Republic, Malaysia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Singapore 30-day; Oman 14-day; Brunei, Philippines, Thailand 14-day (trial). Honduras and Dominican Republic for ordinary passports only. Certain passport holders with specific listed birthplaces are ineligible per BOCA notice.

visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport holders and Certificate of Identity holders; scheme valid until July 31, 2026; proof of accommodation and sufficient funds required
Ordinary passport holders; extendable up to 180 days
Ordinary passport holders only; diplomatic/official passport holders excluded from this scheme
Ordinary passport holders only; diplomatic/official passport holders excluded from this scheme
Ordinary passport holders; scheme valid until March 31, 2030
Ordinary passport holders only; scheme valid until July 31, 2026; proof of accommodation and sufficient funds required
Ordinary passport holders only; scheme valid until July 31, 2026; proof of accommodation and sufficient funds required
Ordinary passport holders; extendable up to 180 days

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Holders of emergency or temporary passports from visa-exempt countries (except USA)
visa_on_arrival.notes: Eligible for landing visa at Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport Bureau of Consular Affairs office. At other airports (Taipei Songshan, Taichung, Kaohsiung), travelers must first obtain a temporary entry permit before applying for a visa at designated MOFA offices. Fee: NT$1,600 (or free for reciprocal agreement countries) + NT$800 handling fee. Not extendable. Stay begins day after arrival.

e_visa.notes: Eligible for eVisa (also has 14-day visa-free access)
Ordinary and special passport holders only; eligibility adjusted per bilateral agreements

e_visa.nationality: Southeast Asian group tourists (Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Vietnam)
e_visa.notes: Must travel with designated travel agencies under the Tourism Administration; group tour requirement; scheme extended through December 31, 2026

e_visa.nationality: Business visitors from Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Iran, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka
e_visa.notes: Must be recommended by Taiwan External Trade Development Council (TAITRA) overseas offices

visa_policy.notes: Taiwan's visa policy is administered by the Bureau of Consular Affairs (BOCA), Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Passport validity of at least 6 months is required for all entries. Time-limited visa-free schemes: Thailand, Brunei, Philippines valid until July 31, 2026 (extended per BOCA announcement https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-220-7850-9cbb1-2.html); North Macedonia until March 31, 2030. Southeast Asian group tourist eVisa scheme extended through December 31, 2026. eVisa max stay days not specified on official page - consult BOCA for current details. The official eVisa application portal is https://visawebapp.boca.gov.tw/BOCA_EVISA/

credential.subtype: participation in sporting events held in Taiwan

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (North Korea) are only eligible for an eVisa when participating in sporting events held in Taiwan. This is an event-specific, activity-conditional eligibility - North Korean nationals cannot obtain a Taiwan eVisa on the basis of nationality alone. Confirmed on the official BOCA eVisa eligibility page.

credential.subtype: any valid visa (electronic visas must be printed)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by the United States (electronic visas must be printed) may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid US resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: permanent resident card expired within 10 years of date of expected arrival

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a US permanent resident card expired within 10 years of the date of expected arrival in Taiwan may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC). Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: any valid visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by Canada may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) Canadian resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by the United Kingdom may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) UK resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by Australia may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) Australian resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by Japan may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan. Note: Indonesian nationals with a valid Japan Visa Waiver Registration are also eligible.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) Japanese resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by the Republic of Korea may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) Korean resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by New Zealand may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) New Zealand resident or permanent resident card may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid entry visa issued by a Schengen Agreement signatory country may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) - multiple-entry, valid 90 days, up to 30 days per stay. Passport must have at least 6 months remaining validity. Must possess onward/return air or ferry ticket. Must never have been employed as a blue-collar worker in Taiwan.

credential.subtype: valid resident or permanent resident card (or expired within 10 years of arrival)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam holding a valid or recently expired (within 10 years) resident or permanent resident card from a Schengen Agreement signatory country may apply for Taiwan Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC).

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa or residence certificate

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Armenia, Colombia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan holding a valid visa or resident certificate issued by a Schengen country are eligible for Taiwan eVisa. Armenia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan eligibility is valid through March 31, 2027.

credential.subtype: valid US resident certificate or visa valid for more than 180 days

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Armenia, Colombia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan holding a valid US resident certificate or visa valid for more than 180 days are eligible for Taiwan eVisa. Armenia, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan eligibility is valid through March 31, 2027.

credential.subtype: prior ROC visitor visa or alien resident certificate issued within the past 10 years (excluding FL/X category visas and foreign laborer ARCs)

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, and Vietnam who previously held a ROC (Taiwan) entry visa or alien resident certificate issued within the past 10 years, with no immigration violations or law-breaking history in Taiwan. Excluded: visas marked 'FL' (foreign laborer) or 'X' (others), and resident cards indicating foreign laborer status. Certificate valid for 90 days, multiple-entry, up to 30 days per stay.

cbi.notes: Taiwan has no formal Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is obtainable through naturalization after 5 years of consecutive legal residency, subject to language proficiency and income requirements. Taiwan generally requires renunciation of prior citizenship unless the applicant holds a reserved occupational qualification. No official government source advertises a CBI product. Source checked: immigration.gov.tw, en.mofa.gov.tw.

programs.name: Resident Visa for Investment
programs.notes: Minimum USD 200,000 investment required as stipulated in 'Regulations on the Operation of Foreign Investors or Representatives of Foreign Corporations Applying for Resident Visas'. Proof of investment issued by competent central government authority required. Processing time: 8 working days for domestic applications.

programs.name: Alien Permanent Resident Certificate (Plum Blossom Card) - Business Investment
programs.notes: Investment of NTD 15 million in a profitable ROC business, creating employment for 5 or more ROC nationals, maintained for 3 consecutive years. Not subject to 183-day/year residency requirement. Application fee: NTD 10,000. Dual ROC citizenship not permitted.

programs.name: Alien Permanent Resident Certificate (Plum Blossom Card) - Government Bonds
programs.notes: Purchase of ROC central government bonds with face value exceeding NTD 30 million, held for 3 consecutive years. Not subject to 183-day/year residency requirement. Application fee: NTD 10,000.

programs.name: Entrepreneur Visa (Startup/Business Founder Residence)
programs.notes: Initial 2-year validity, extendable in 2-year increments. For renewal: annual gross revenue of at least NTD 3 million OR average over 3 years, OR operating expenses at least NTD 1 million/year, OR employment of 3+ full-time ROC nationals. Processing: 30 working days initial, 22 working days for extension. Apply via Foreign Professionals Online Application Platform.

programs.name: Employment Gold Card
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with demonstrated professional expertise in one of 12 designated fields: Architecture, Biotechnology, Culture and Arts, Digital, Economy, Education, Environment, Finance, Law, National Defense, Science & Technology, Sports. Applicants must meet field-specific salary thresholds or hold recognized qualifications.
programs.processing_time: Joint review by National Immigration Agency, Ministry of Labor, Ministry of Foreign Affairs, and specialized authorities
programs.notes: Combined open work permit, resident visa, Alien Resident Certificate, and re-entry permit valid up to 3 years. Open work authorization (work for any employer). Special tax incentives for high earners. Apply via Foreign Professionals Online Application Platform at https://coa.immigration.gov.tw/coa-frontend/four-in-one/entry/golden-card. Official site also at foreigntalentact.ndc.gov.tw.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visitor Visa
programs.eligibility: Nationals of Taiwan visa-exempt countries only. Age 30+: annual salary at least USD 40,000 (within last 2 years); Age 20-29: annual salary at least USD 20,000. Alternative: hold a digital nomad visa from another country. Bank statements must show average at least USD 10,000/month over 6 months. Must hold proof of international health insurance.
programs.processing_time: Applications submitted to ROC overseas mission, forwarded to National Development Council for review; or domestic applications at BOCA/MOFA offices at least 10 working days before stay expires
programs.notes: Program launched per Act for the Recruitment and Employment of Foreign Professionals. Maximum stay duration and renewal period not specified verbatim on BOCA's official page. Also available via: https://foreigntalentact.ndc.gov.tw and https://digitalnomad.ndc.gov.tw/. Program information also at https://talent.nat.gov.tw/visa/digital-nomad

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Tourism
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals wishing to visit Taiwan for tourism, sightseeing, or leisure purposes.
visa_types.notes: Stay options of 14, 30, 60, or 90 days available. Validity generally 3 months to 1 year. Single-entry fee US$50; multiple-entry US$100. Extensions possible at NIA for 60- and 90-day stays (up to 120 and 90 additional days respectively). Online application at visawebapp.boca.gov.tw. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Business
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals conducting commercial business activities in Taiwan, including meetings, negotiations, and trade activities.
visa_types.notes: Stay options of 14, 30, 60, or 90 days. Evidence of business activities required (employment letter, invitation letter, or trading records). For nationals of designated countries: guarantor documentation required with NT$50/page cable fee. Single-entry US$50, multiple-entry US$100.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Employment
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have obtained an approved work permit from Taiwan's Ministry of Labor and intend to take up employment in Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Requires Approval Letter issued by Taiwan Ministry of Labor prior to visa application. Applicants typically convert to Resident Visa after arrival. Employers must obtain work permits before visa application.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - White Collar Worker
visa_types.purpose: For skilled professional foreign workers taking up employment in Taiwan with an approved work permit, allowing residence for the duration of employment.
visa_types.notes: Resident visa validity 3 months; holder must apply for Alien Resident Certificate (ARC) within 30 days of arrival at NIA. Stay duration tied to ARC validity. Single-entry resident visa NT$2,200 / US$66; multiple-entry NT$4,400 / US$132. Domestic conversion from visitor visa incurs additional NT$800 handling fee.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Full-time Academic Student
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals admitted to a full-time degree program (secondary, bachelor's, master's, or doctoral) at a recognized Taiwan educational institution.
visa_types.notes: Admission letter from a recognized Taiwan university or school required. Must apply for ARC within 30 days of arrival. Stay tied to ARC/enrollment validity.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Studying Chinese
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled in Mandarin Chinese language courses at approved language centers in Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Enrollment documentation from approved Mandarin language center required. Extensions available through NIA.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Visitor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For remote workers from visa-exempt countries who wish to live in Taiwan while working for a foreign employer or as a freelancer.
visa_types.notes: Only nationals of Taiwan visa-exempt countries are eligible. Age 30+: annual salary of at least USD 40,000 in last 2 years; age 20-29: at least USD 20,000. Alternative: hold a digital nomad visa from another country. Bank statements must show average monthly balance of at least USD 10,000 over 6 months. International health insurance covering full hospitalization required. Applications outside Taiwan submitted to ROC overseas missions; those already in Taiwan must apply at BOCA/MOFA at least 10 working days before current stay expires. Max stay and validity period not stated verbatim on official page.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Japanese Retirees (Age 55+)
visa_types.purpose: A special long-stay visitor visa for Japanese nationals aged 55 and older who wish to spend retirement time in Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Exclusive to Japanese nationals. Two options: 1-year multiple-entry visa (requires insurance valid at least 2 years) or 6-month multiple-entry visa (insurance valid 6 months to 2 years). Each stay is non-extendable and up to 180 days. Requires proof of financial assets of at least USD 50,000 plus pension documentation. Police clearance certificate required. Accompanying spouse has no age requirement.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Investment
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals making a qualifying investment in Taiwan who intend to reside in Taiwan to manage their investment.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment of USD 200,000. Proof of investment issued by competent central government authority required. Visa validity 3 months; ARC must be applied for within 30 days of arrival. Processing: 8 working days for domestic applications.

visa_types.name: Entrepreneur Visa (Startup Resident Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who establish or join a startup company in Taiwan and meet recognized entrepreneurial criteria.
visa_types.notes: Initial 2-year validity, extendable in 2-year increments. Renewal criteria: annual gross revenue NTD 3M+, OR average revenue over 3 years, OR operating expenses NTD 1M+/year, OR employment of 3+ full-time ROC nationals. Processing 30 working days initial; 22 working days for extensions. Apply via Foreign Professionals Online Application Platform.

visa_types.name: Employment Gold Card
visa_types.purpose: A combined open work permit, resident visa, Alien Resident Certificate, and re-entry permit for highly skilled foreign professionals in 11 designated fields.
visa_types.notes: Valid 1, 2, or 3 years (applicant's choice). Eligible fields: Architecture, Arts and Culture, Digital, Economy, Education, Finance, National Defense, Science and Technology, Law, Sports, Biotechnology/Environment. Fees vary: NT$3,700-NT$5,700 (most foreign passports); US passport holders abroad pay NT$7,605-NT$9,605. Process can exceed 60 business days. Open work authorization - work for any employer. Special tax incentives available. Apply at coa.immigration.gov.tw.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa
visa_types.purpose: For young nationals of eligible countries to travel and work in Taiwan for up to one year, experiencing Taiwanese culture and supplementing travel funds through employment.
visa_types.notes: Eligible countries: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Czech Republic, France, Germany, Hungary, Ireland, Japan, South Korea, New Zealand, Poland, Slovakia. Israel suspended as of May 2025. Age limits vary by country (typically 18-30 or 18-35). Specific requirements per country available via individual country guideline documents on BOCA site.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Employment-Seeking
visa_types.purpose: For qualified foreign professionals seeking employment opportunities in Taiwan, allowing them to job-search during their stay.
visa_types.notes: Eligibility: average monthly salary over past 6 months of at least NT$47,971; OR recent graduate (within 1 year) from a world top-1,500 university and currently unemployed; OR deemed eligible by MOFA. Requires financial proof of at least NT$100,000, health/hospitalization insurance, certificate of good conduct, and employment-seeking plan. Passport with 6+ months validity required.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Foreign Spouse of ROC Citizen
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals married to ROC citizens to reside in Taiwan for family reunification.
visa_types.notes: Two sub-categories: Foreign Spouses of ROC Citizens with Household Registration, and without Household Registration (also covers Hong Kong/Macau/mainland Chinese residents via separate rules). Marriage certificate and supporting documents required. ARC must be obtained within 30 days of arrival.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Minors Joining Family
visa_types.purpose: For foreign minors under age 18 joining parents or grandparents residing in Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Requires proof of parent's or grandparent's legal residence in Taiwan. ARC must be obtained within 30 days of arrival.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Medical Treatment
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Taiwan to receive medical treatment for diseases on the designated 28 Disease Item List for Foreign Nationals at recognized Taiwan medical institutions.
visa_types.notes: Must provide diagnosis and treatment proposal from a Taiwan medical institution and proof of funds. Up to 2 accompanying family members (spouse or relatives within third degree) and 2 medical personnel from home country allowed. Urgent cases can be initiated directly by Taiwan medical institutions.

visa_types.name: Landing Visa (Transit/Emergency)
visa_types.purpose: For nationals of visa-exempt countries holding emergency or temporary passports who need to enter Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Only for holders of emergency or temporary passports from visa-exempt countries (USA excluded). Fee: NT$1,600 (or gratis for reciprocal agreement countries) plus NT$800 handling fee (approximately US$24 additional). Stay non-extendable, begins day after arrival. Available at Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport (on-site BOCA office). Other airports (Songshan, Taichung, Kaohsiung) require temporary entry permit first. Cannot be converted to visitor or resident visa under normal circumstances.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Volunteering
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals participating in approved volunteer programs in Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Volunteer program documentation and approval required. Single-entry fee US$50.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Conference and Exhibition
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals attending international conferences, exhibitions, or trade shows held in Taiwan.
visa_types.notes: Invitation letter from conference or exhibition organizer required. Also available as eVisa for eligible nationalities attending international conferences.

visa_types.name: Resident Visa - Official Duties / International Exchange
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals serving in official capacities or participating in international exchange programs approved by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
visa_types.notes: Official letters of approval from competent authority required. Gratis issuance for reciprocal agreement countries. Diplomatic and official passport holders may have separate exemption arrangements.

validation_notes: CORRECTED (2025-05-12): Dominican Republic max_stay_days was erroneously listed under the 90-day visa_free group in the prior record. Official BOCA page confirms Dominican Republic ordinary passport holders receive 30 days, not 90. Record now correctly shows 30 days.

CORRECTED (2025-05-12): Brunei notes updated to include 'Certificate of Identity holders' as eligible, per live BOCA page language ('ordinary passports and Certificate of Identity holders'). Prior record stated 'ordinary passports' only.

CORRECTED (2025-05-12): Landing visa (visa_on_arrival) notes updated to clarify airport-specific procedure: Taoyuan Airport has on-site BOCA office; other airports (Songshan, Taichung, Kaohsiung) require temporary entry permit first, then visa at MOFA offices.

CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): Southeast Asian group tourist eVisa scheme extended through December 31, 2026 (per BOCA announcement https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-220-7850-9cbb1-2.html); added this to group tourist eVisa notes.
CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): Thailand, Brunei, Philippines visa-free scheme extended to July 31, 2026 (per BOCA announcement). Dates in prior record were correct.
CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): Armenia, Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan eVisa via credential valid through March 31, 2027 - unchanged.
CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): Colombia eVisa via Schengen or US credential - unchanged; no expiry date specified on BOCA page.
CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): All 90-day, 30-day, 14-day visa-free nationality lists match the live BOCA page. No new countries added or removed as of validation date.
CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): Honduras correctly listed at 90 days (ordinary passports only).
CONFIRMED (2025-05-12): Oman dual-listed (14-day visa-free and eVisa eligible) - confirmed from live BOCA pages.

ADDED (2026-06-27): North Korea (PRK) added to e_visa list - confirmed on live BOCA eVisa eligibility page (https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-181-8051-69fbd-2.html) as eligible only when participating in sporting events in Taiwan. Entry is event-specific/conditional; not an unconditional eVisa grant. Added with explicit notes reflecting this restriction.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): Full visa-free list re-fetched from live BOCA page (https://www.boca.gov.tw/fp-149-4486-7785a-2.html and https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-149-4486-7785a-2.html). No new countries added or removed from visa-free, visa-on-arrival, or eVisa lists since prior validation. All stay durations and expiry dates verified unchanged.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): Thailand, Brunei, Philippines 14-day visa-free schemes still active with July 31, 2026 expiry - confirmed from live BOCA announcement page.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): Southeast Asian group tourist eVisa scheme still extended through December 31, 2026 - confirmed from live BOCA announcement page.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): TAC (Travel Authorization Certificate) program for Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, Vietnam remains active; credential requirements unchanged (US/Canada/UK/Australia/Japan/Korea/NZ visa or residence, Schengen visa or residence, or prior ROC visa within 10 years).

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): eVisa unconditional list (Bahrain, BIH, Dominica, Ecuador, Haiti, Kuwait, Mauritius, Montenegro, Oman, Panama, Peru, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Türkiye, UAE) - all confirmed on live BOCA eVisa page. Official page now uses spelling 'Türkiye' (not 'Turkey'); corrected in this note.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): UK and Canada passport holders noted on live BOCA page as able to extend 90-day visa-exempt stay up to 180 days total - already reflected in notes for those entries.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): Full re-verification against live BOCA official pages (https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-149-4486-7785a-2.html, https://www.boca.gov.tw/fp-149-4486-7785a-2.html, https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-181-8051-69fbd-2.html, https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-220-1885-b6ae7-2.html, https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-220-7850-9cbb1-2.html). No additions, removals, or stay-duration changes detected across visa-free, visa_on_arrival, eVisa, or conditional_access lists. All data confirmed unchanged from prior 2026-06-27 validation.

ALERT (2026-06-29): Thailand, Brunei, and Philippines 14-day visa-free trial schemes expire July 31, 2026 — approximately 32 days from this validation date. BOCA has not yet announced a further extension. These entries should be re-verified against live BOCA pages on or after August 1, 2026 and moved to visa_required or updated if renewed.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): Kuwait confirmed present on live BOCA eVisa eligibility page (complete list includes Kuwait without asterisk/conditional notation). Record correctly includes Kuwait in unconditional eVisa list.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): TAC program (conditional_access credential-based entries for Cambodia, India, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, Vietnam) confirmed unchanged - same qualifying issuers (US, Canada, UK, Australia, Japan, Korea, New Zealand, Schengen) and conditions per live BOCA page.
gaps: eVisa max_stay_days not stated verbatim on official BOCA page (https://www.boca.gov.tw/cp-181-8051-69fbd-2.html); omitted per hard rules
Digital Nomad Visa max stay and renewal period not stated verbatim on official BOCA page; omitted per hard rules
Global Elite Card (announced by NDC for NTD 6M+ annual earners with 1-year PR path) not yet confirmed enacted into law with an active official program page; omitted
Diplomatic/official/service passport visa-exemption list mirrors ordinary passport list; distinct stay durations by passport type not confirmed from official source text
Taiwan has no formal freedom-of-movement / common-travel-area memberships; agreements array is empty
Citizenship naturalization via investment path (APRC then 5-year naturalization) not confirmed verbatim from a single official page; path_to_citizenship_years for RBI programs left null accordingly
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific Taiwan entry privilege confirmed on any official BOCA or NIA page

Southeast Asian group tourist eVisa - Philippines omitted from this category (Philippines now has visa-free 14-day access); whether Philippines nationals in a group tour context could use this eVisa route instead is not clarified on the official page
North Korea (PRK) eVisa for sporting events - max_stay_days not specified on BOCA page; added to e_visa list with event-specific condition note but stay duration unknown

International conference/sporting event/trade fair eVisa (Ecode-based, open to any nationality) - listed on BOCA eVisa page as a special category; not added to e_visa visa_policy list as it is event-conditional and not a standing nationality-based grant; flagged as gap for future consideration

EXPIRY WATCH (2026-06-29): Thailand (THA), Brunei (BRN), Philippines (PHL) 14-day visa-free trial schemes expire July 31, 2026. Re-verify on/after August 1, 2026. If not renewed, these three entries must be removed from visa_policy.visa_free.
